steps.text: Prepare piranha solution by slowly adding 30 mL of 30% hydrogen peroxide to 90 mL of concentrated sulfuric acid in a glass beaker inside the fume hood.
Submerge glass coverslips in the piranha solution for 15 minutes to strip organic residue.
Rinse the coverslips thoroughly with deionized water and dry under a stream of nitrogen.
Pour the spent piranha solution into the acid waste carboy.
Rinse the glassware used for the protein purification buffer (PBS with 0.02% sodium azide) and add that rinse to the same acid waste carboy.

recognized_mixtures.reasoning: Piranha solution is the well-known name for the mixture formed by combining hydrogen peroxide and concentrated sulfuric acid in step 1; it is not an independently sourced chemical but the resulting mixture of c1 and c2.

The 'protein purification buffer' is explicitly described as 'PBS with 0.02% sodium azide', i.e., a named solution composed of the buffer PBS (c5) and the preservative sodium azide (c6), rather than a separate chemical to look up.
